package-as-trembus-project-schema.title: Package as trembus project-schema
package-as-trembus-project-schema.caption: Where the packaging plan stands today.

authoring-loop.title: Authoring loop
authoring-loop.caption: How every _project/ artifact is born valid and reaches the dashboard.

steps.label: Run /new <kind>
steps.note: Pick a kind from the config (decision · report · pipeline · roadmap · session · workflow · feature). The kind is validated against the config, so there are no per-kind commands to maintain.

steps.label: Scaffold file
steps.note: Derives the filename per the kind's scheme, writes valid frontmatter (status = the kind's initialStatus), lays down the section skeleton, and self-validates — conformant by construction.

steps.label: Edit title · status · links
steps.note: Fill the authored surface — title, status, optional links/tags. id and kind are derived (folder + filename), never authored.

steps.label: Guard the save
steps.note: The hook re-runs the single validateEntity check on every _project/ write and blocks (exit 2) anything that would break the contract. Read-only, fails open.

steps.label: Validate the graph
steps.note: Runs the same check across the whole graph — frontmatter, link integrity, prose↔status agreement. Also the SessionStart advisory health summary.

steps.label: Emit JSON contract
steps.note: Emits graph.json + hub.json from _project/. A Vite dev plugin, not a hook — editing any _project/ file repaints the dashboard.

steps.label: Render live
steps.note: The Command Center renders the emitted contract; with vite dev running it hot-reloads on every _project/ edit.

instantiate-a-new-project-system-project.title: Instantiate a new Project-System project
instantiate-a-new-project-system-project.caption: From an empty repo to a verified, ready-to-author Project-System consumer.

steps.label: Vendor .project-system/
steps.detail: copy schema · lib · tools verbatim — never edit/rename
steps.note: Copy schema/ · lib/ · tools/ into a reserved .project-system/ at the project root. Never edit or rename anything inside it — updating the framework = re-copy the folder.

steps.label: Run setup-project-system
steps.note: The setup-project-system skill runs the naming-convention interview and assembles a config spec, then drives init-config.mjs.

steps.label: Generate born-valid config
steps.detail: init-config.mjs · proven to load via contract before write
steps.note: init-config.mjs fills conventional defaults and proves the config loads through lib/contract.mjs before writing it — born valid, like a scaffolded entity.

refs.title: Generate consumer configs with a born-valid init tool and setup skill

steps.label: Copy templates/consumer/.claude
steps.note: Copy templates/consumer/.claude verbatim: the blocking PreToolUse guard, the advisory SessionStart health summary, the generic /new command, and the setup skill. The wiring is identical for every consumer.

steps.label: Register the consumer
steps.note: Add the project to check-consumer-drift.mjs (schema · root · config, plus claudeDir for hook-parity) so the mirror stays honest until the framework is published.

steps.label: Verify the mirror
steps.note: Run check-consumer-drift.mjs (or npm test from the framework) to confirm the vendored contract, validator, and hooks match the canonical source.

steps.label: Author first entity
steps.note: From here the authoring loop takes over — /new <kind> scaffolds your first born-valid artifact.

runs.label: At the adoption gate
runs.note: Mirror holding green; waiting on a 3rd independent consumer before publishing.

runs.label: Mirror shipped
runs.note: Contract mirrored with the CI drift check; nothing downstream has started.

command-center.label: Freeze the contract
command-center.detail: graph.json + hub.json adopted as the input interface, spec'd in docs/spec/, with render-hub --check in CI.

command-center.label: Scaffold the island
command-center.detail: apps/command-center — Vite + React 19, its own lockfile/node_modules; never leaks a dependency into the zero-dep core.

command-center.label: Render the graph
command-center.detail: Lineage view built, then removed 2026-06-25 ('remove the Graph section for now'); buildGraphContract + the @trembus/viz dep stay for an easy revival.

command-center.label: The three boards
command-center.detail: Hub (Overview) — now with control-surface tiles (commands · workflows · hooks) + a right-side details drawer — and the Plan-Progress board are in; the Workflows tab replays swimlanes from _project/ data. The Decision-Tree board is still to do.

command-center.label: Live reload
command-center.detail: ✅ 2026-06-26 — a dev-only Vite plugin (apply:'serve') watches _project/** and re-runs render-hub on each edit; the static JSON import means Vite repaints on the rewritten contract. vite dev is now the launch.json target; build/preview still read the committed JSON.

command-center.label: Supersede the static HTML
command-center.detail: ✅ 2026-06-25 — the live Hub renders hub.json, so render-hub emits JSON only.

command-center.label: Data-driven kinds
command-center.detail: ✅ 2026-06-27 — render-hub emits per-kind tone + swimlaneKinds + a nav passthrough; the app derives presentation from the contract, so adding a kind is config + folder with zero app edits (decisions 0006 workflow-kind + 0007 data-driven). The control-surface hub tiles and the migrated authoring-loop workflow landed here too.

root.label: Project-system
root.brief: A first-principles, project-agnostic framework for the planning layer of any project. One contract — ProjectEntity — that every planning artifact derives from, plus the zero-dependency tooling that validates, scaffolds, guards, and renders it. This guide is itself derived from the contract: the framework anatomy below is authored, the _project/ surface and conventions are generated live from this project's config, so the guide can't drift from reality.

children.label: .project-system/ — the vendored core
children.brief: How a consuming project carries the framework: vendor schema/ + lib/ + tools/ verbatim into a reserved .project-system/ at the project root, and never edit or rename a file inside it — updating means re-copying the folder. The only project-specific file an adopter writes is project-system.config.json at the root. This core is universal: identical in every project.

children.label: schema/
children.brief: The contract layer — machine-readable JSON Schema. Holds the universal base contract every consumer mirrors, and the meta-schema that validates a project's config.

children.label: project-entity.base.schema.json
children.brief: The universal contract: the three primitives plus the link/rel definitions. Identical across every consuming project — the artifact a consumer mirrors, and what the drift check compares against.

children.label: project-config.schema.json
children.brief: The meta-schema: validates a project's project-system.config.json. The only project-specific file an adopter authors is checked against this.

children.label: lib/
children.brief: The shared library every engine reads through — a zero-dependency parser plus the seam that composes the base schema with a project's config. Single source: no tool re-implements either.

children.label: md.mjs
children.brief: Zero-dependency frontmatter/markdown parser — the single source all tools read frontmatter and body sections through, so they can never drift on what a file says.

children.label: contract.mjs
children.brief: The seam between the project-agnostic engines and a project's specifics. loadContract() composes base schema + config into the runtime `ctx` every engine consumes; loadEntities() reads the _project/ tree, deriving kind from the folder and id from the filename.

children.label: tools/
children.brief: The zero-dependency engines, all reading the one contract via lib/contract.mjs — no check is re-implemented in a second place. The validator is the single check the others reuse.

children.label: validate.mjs
children.brief: The validator + validateEntity — the single check (per-kind enums, link resolution, prose ↔ frontmatter) that the scaffolder, guard, and renderer all reuse.

children.label: new-entity.mjs
children.brief: The scaffolder behind the single /new <kind> command. Validates the kind against your config and writes valid frontmatter + the kind's scaffold sections, so there are no per-kind commands to hand-maintain.

children.label: guard.mjs
children.brief: The PreToolUse guard — the only hook that can block. Rejects any _project/ write that would break the contract (exit 2 + reason); read-only and fails open.

children.label: render-hub.mjs
children.brief: The renderer. Projects the validated entity graph into the two JSON contracts the Command Center consumes (graph.json + hub.json) — including this very guide. Domain-neutral end to end.

children.label: check-consumer-drift.mjs
children.brief: The packaging discipline: asserts each consumer mirrors the canonical contract (schema, validator behavior, and hook parity). The gate that keeps the un-published mirror honest.

children.label: the two hooks
children.brief: The contract is enforced by exactly two Claude Code hooks: a BLOCKING PreToolUse(Write|Edit) guard (tools/guard.mjs — the only hook that can block; exit 2 + reason, read-only, fails open) and an ADVISORY SessionStart health summary (validate.mjs --summary, always exit 0). Rendering is NOT a hook — it's the Command Center's Vite dev plugin. Consumers vendor these verbatim.

children.label: _project/ — planning surface
children.brief: The project's planning surface — every file here is exactly one ProjectEntity. There is one subfolder per declared kind, and the subtree below is generated from THIS project's project-system.config.json, so it reflects exactly the kinds, folders, naming schemes, and status enums this project declares. kind is derived from the folder; id from the filename; the authored surface of a file is just title · status · updated (+ optional links · tags).

children.label: decisions/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "decision". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the serial · 4-digit scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "proposed" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: 0001-example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a decision (synthesized from the serial · 4-digit scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: reports/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "report". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "draft" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: 2026-06-29-example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a report (synthesized from the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: pipeline/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "pipeline". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "design" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a pipeline (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: roadmap/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "roadmap". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "proposed" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a roadmap (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: sessions/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "session". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "planned" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: 2026-06-29-example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a session (synthesized from the date-slug · <date>-<slug> scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: workflows/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "workflow". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "draft" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a workflow (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: features/
children.brief: Holds entities of kind "feature". kind is derived from this folder, id from each filename. Files are named by the slug scheme; a freshly scaffolded file is born "planned" and its status ranges over the per-kind enum below.

children.label: example-title.md
children.brief: A representative filename for a feature (synthesized from the slug scheme) — not a live entity. The filename stem becomes the entity id.

children.label: The three primitives
children.brief: Everything reduces to three primitives — Identity (kind · id · title) · State (status · updated) · Relation (links[]). kind and id are loader-derived from the file's path; the rest is the authored frontmatter. Everything else a file might carry is an optional `tags` facet. A good reduction deletes fields; this one dropped many per-kind interfaces to one shape.

children.label: Relation vocabulary
children.brief: The universal vocabulary for the typed edges between entities (links[].rel), defined once in the base schema and shared by every project. WHICH kinds each rel may point at is this project's config (relTargetKinds) — the facts below are that resolution. A link target is a `<folder>/<id>` path, a milestone marker, or an external ref.
